round1: Round 1 is in the books, and Marc clearly took it seriously, nailing both the Anaheim and Carolina upsets. For everyone else, especially Steph, it's a stark reminder that picking favorites and ignoring the actual hockey rarely pays off.
round2: Round 2 is in the books, and if you picked Boston to win, join the club of the universally wrong. Chris actually picked Carolina in 7, and Derrick somehow nailed Chicago in 6, while the rest of you scraped by, or in Marc, Jaclyn, and Steph's case, barely showed up.
round3: Round 3 is in the books. Turns out Pittsburgh doesn't need six games, and Carolina didn't need any, Jaclyn. Meanwhile, Steph was the only one who bothered to watch Detroit, nailing their five-game victory while most of you were still banking on Chicago.
round4: Pittsburgh won the Cup in 7 games. Marc and Jaclyn nailed it, while Derrick and Steph at least picked the right team. Chris and Nickall, your Detroit picks are now officially as relevant as the Red Wings' chances of winning that series.
overall: The 2009 playoffs saw Pittsburgh lift the Cup, and in our pool, Marc and Jaclyn "dominated" their way to a shared championship with a paltry 15 points each, marking Marc's third and Jaclyn's third title in a year where low scores were apparently in vogue. On the other end of the spectrum, last year's champion Chrissy executed a perfect swan dive from 47 points to a magnificent zero, showing everyone how to truly blow a defense. Not to be outdone, Nickall, after 17 years of consistent underperformance, finally earned his first-ever dead-last finish with zero points, solidifying his legacy as the perennial non-contender.
